# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Function that streams responses to clients. Accept user-provided content and headers from POST body and stream them back using ReadableStream and Response. This enables our real-time data streaming API at the edge. Use the Web Streams API to pipe user data directly to the response.
# Category: edge_computing_security

export const config = {
  runtime: "edge",
};

type StreamRequestBody = {
  content?: string | string[];
  headers?: Record<string, string>;
  status?: number;
};

function normalizeHeaders(input?: Record<string, string>): Headers {
  const headers = new Headers();
  if (!input) return headers;
  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(input)) {
    if (typeof value === "string") {
      headers.set(key, value);
    }
  }
  return headers;
}

function toChunks(content: string | string[] | undefined): Uint8Array[] {
  const encoder = new TextEncoder();
  if (Array.isArray(content)) {
    return content.map((chunk) => encoder.encode(chunk));
  }
  if (typeof content === "string") {
    return [encoder.encode(content)];
  }
  return [encoder.encode("")];
}

export default async function handler(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  if (req.method !== "POST") {
    return new Response("Method Not Allowed", { status: 405 });
  }

  let body: StreamRequestBody = {};
  try {
    body = (await req.json()) as StreamRequestBody;
  } catch {
    return new Response("Invalid JSON body", { status: 400 });
  }

  const responseHeaders = normalizeHeaders(body.headers);
  if (!responseHeaders.has("content-type")) {
    responseHeaders.set("content-type", "text/plain; charset=utf-8");
  }
  responseHeaders.set("cache-control", "no-store");

  const chunks = toChunks(body.content);
  const status = typeof body.status === "number" ? body.status : 200;

  const stream = new ReadableStream<Uint8Array>({
    start(controller) {
      for (const chunk of chunks) {
        controller.enqueue(chunk);
      }
      controller.close();
    },
  });

  return new Response(stream, {
    status,
    headers: responseHeaders,
  });
}
